{"query":"Jannik Sinner 2026 Wimbledon final match score opponent","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://sports.yahoo.com/tennis/live/wimbledon-mens-final-jannik-sinner-repeats-as-champion-after-weathering-early-challenge-from-alexander-zverev-140000546.html","title":"Wimbledon men's final: Jannik Sinner repeats as champion after weathering early challenge from Alexander Zverev - Yahoo Sports","content":"# Wimbledon men's final: Jannik Sinner repeats as champion after weathering early challenge from Alexander Zverev. Jannik Sinner experienced a different Alexander Zverev in the 2026 Wimbledon men's final. Facing an opponent playing some of the best tennis of a decorated career, Sinner held steady on his way to a 6-7 (7), 7-6 (2), 6-3, 6-4 win to repeat as Wimbledon champion. For a player who is usually unflappable when the weather agrees with him, it was an odd run, especially with him making history by winning all five Masters 1000 titles between March and May. This tournament got off to a shaky start as well, with Sinner having to survive in five sets against Miomir Kecmanović with his foot bleeding through his shoe. The basic breakdown of Zverev's game is something like this: At 6-foot-6, he is one of the hardest servers in the sport and can rack up easy points.","score":0.9220975,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=43AC1PNAr_8","title":"Wimbledon 2026 Men’s Singles Final LIVE: Jannik Sinner vs. Alexander Zverev Final Live Score | N18G","content":"Wimbledon 2026 Men’s Singles Final LIVE: Jannik Sinner vs. Alexander Zverev Final Live Score | N18G

Wimbledon 2026 Men’s Singles Final LIVE: Jannik Sinner vs. Alexander Zverev Final Live Score | 2026 Wimbledon men's final LIVE | CNBC TV18
Source: Royal Family via Reuters/ IMAGN IMAGES via Reuters Connect
The 2026 Men’s Singles Final at the Wimbledon is locked in as defending champion Jannik Sinner is set to take on the reigning Roland Garros champion Alexander Zverev on Sunday at the Centre Court.
The final was set up after Alexander Zverev defeated the homegrown wildcard Arthur Fery in straight sets (7-6 6-2 6-4) and Sinner demolished the legendary Novak Djokovic, with a commanding 6-4 6-4 6-4) win.
Zverev had never made a Wimbledon quarterfinal before this year and is now in the final after a flawless performance against Fery. However, his kryptonite, Sinner is looking his best again after a disappointing early exit at the Roland Garros and will be in contention for a successive Wimbledon win. It’s a 15th career showdown between the two top seeds, and a second Grand Slam final meeting, with Sinner having beaten Zverev at the Australian Open in January of last year. Sinner is labelled as Zverev’s kryptonite because he has defeated him nine consecutive times across every surface since 2024 with Zverev’s last win against him coming back in the US Open 2023.
